description: Engine Foundation & Player — Three.js + ECS engine mounted at /play inside the existing Next.js 16 + T3 + WorkOS AuthKit + Drizzle/Neon foundation. Adds movement, isometric camera, character creation, the game-table Drizzle schema, and the gameRouter tRPC endpoints. Auth, env validation, tRPC bootstrap, and the Drizzle/Neon client are already in place — do NOT redo them.

userStories.title: Install Three.js & mount the game canvas at /play
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Three.js installed and a client-only canvas component mounted inside the protected /play route so the engine can run in the browser without breaking SSR.
acceptanceCriteria: three and @types/three installed (pnpm add three && pnpm add -D @types/three)
src/app/play/_components/GameCanvas.tsx is a 'use client' component that owns a <canvas> element, sizes it to its parent, and instantiates the Game orchestrator (US-017) on mount
src/app/play/page.tsx imports GameCanvas via next/dynamic(() => import('./_components/GameCanvas'), { ssr: false }) so Three.js never executes during server rendering
GameCanvas calls game.destroy() on unmount — no leaked WebGL contexts during HMR or route changes
/play page still uses withAuth({ ensureSignedIn: true }) and passes a safe subset of the WorkOS user (e.g. { id, firstName, email }) down as a prop
pnpm dev renders a Three.js scene (colored box on a ground plane) inside /play for an authenticated user
pnpm typecheck and pnpm build pass
userStories.notes: next/dynamic with ssr:false must live in a 'use client' component in Next.js 16+; added GameLoader.tsx as the client wrapper.

userStories.title: Core engine types and centralized config
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared type definitions, game event types, and a centralized config file so all modules reference a single source of truth for game constants.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/types.ts defines: GameAction enum (Jump, Interact, ToggleMap, ToggleDebug, Pause), CatType enum (Loaf, Zoomies, CuriosityCat, Pounce), TerrainType enum, GameEvent discriminated union, Vector3-compatible types

src/game/config.ts contains all GDD constants: walk speed 4.5 u/s, jump impulse 3.5u, gravity -12 u/s^2, coyote time 5 frames, jump buffer 5 frames, collision radius 0.4u, accel 0.3s, decel 0.2s, swimming speeds, oxygen rates, camera angles (45deg azimuth, 60deg elevation), auto-save interval 30s
Config exported as frozen CONFIG plus a mutable runtimeConfig deep-copy for the debug menu
All game source lives under src/game/ (engine, ecs, systems, ui, maps subfolders) — kept cleanly separate from src/app/, src/server/, src/trpc/
pnpm typecheck passes
userStories.notes: GameAction, CatType, TerrainType enums; Vec2/Vec3 interfaces; GameEvent discriminated union in types.ts. CONFIG frozen + runtimeConfig mutable copy in config.ts.

userStories.title: EventBus typed pub/sub system
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a typed event bus so modules communicate without direct coupling.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/engine/EventBus.ts implements emit<T>(event), on<T>(type, handler): Unsubscribe, off(type, handler)
Events typed against the GameEvent discriminated union from US-002
Multiple listeners per event type supported
Unsubscribe returned by on() removes that specific listener
Destroyed modules can clean up subscriptions (no leaks across HMR cycles)
pnpm typecheck passes
userStories.notes: EventBus uses Map<string, Set<AnyHandler>>. Internal storage uses (event: GameEvent) => void with unknown-cast boundary to satisfy TS strict contravariance. clear() enables leak-free teardown across HMR.

userStories.title: SceneManager — Three.js isolation boundary
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Three.js isolated behind a single module so no game logic ever imports three directly.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/engine/SceneManager.ts exposes constructor(canvas), addMesh(config): SceneHandle, removeMesh(handle), updateTransform(handle, position, rotation, scale), render(), resize(width, height), screenToWorld(screenX, screenY): Vector3 | null, dispose()
MeshConfig supports geometries box, sphere, cylinder, plane plus size, color (hex string), castShadow, receiveShadow
Internally creates THREE.Scene, THREE.WebGLRenderer, THREE.AmbientLight, THREE.DirectionalLight
Handles ResizeObserver / window resize events and device pixel ratio
SceneHandle is opaque — callers cannot reach the underlying THREE.Object3D
Only SceneManager and CameraController may import from three (enforced by ESLint no-restricted-imports in US-019)
dispose() releases all GPU resources — required for clean HMR / route changes
pnpm typecheck passes
userStories.notes: SceneHandle is a Symbol (opaque). MeshConfig supports box/sphere/cylinder/plane. camera is a public settable field so CameraController can install an OrthographicCamera. screenToWorld() uses Raycaster against scene meshes. dispose() removes ResizeObserver + all mesh GPU resources.

userStories.title: ECS World, Entity, Component, and System base types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an Entity-Component-System foundation so all game objects are data-driven and systems operate on component queries.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/ecs/Entity.ts — Entity is a numeric ID type alias (not a class)
src/game/ecs/Component.ts — base component interface with a static type tag for identification
src/game/ecs/System.ts — System interface with update(world, dt)

src/game/ecs/World.ts implements createEntity(): Entity, destroyEntity(entity), addComponent<T>(entity, component), removeComponent<T>(entity, type), getComponent<T>(entity, type): T | null, query(...types): Entity[]
query() results cached; second call with same component types is O(1). Cache invalidates on add/remove.
pnpm typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Entity is a numeric type alias. Component is an interface with a `type: string` tag. System interface has update(world, dt). World uses Map<Entity, Map<ComponentType, Component>> with a sorted-key query cache that invalidates on any structural change.

userStories.title: ECS core components — Transform, Renderable, Velocity, Collider, PlayerControlled
userStories.description: As a developer, I need core ECS components so entities can have position, visuals, physics, and player control.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/ecs/components/Transform.ts — position (x,y,z), rotation (y-axis radians), scale (x,y,z)
src/game/ecs/components/Renderable.ts — meshConfig (from SceneManager), sceneHandle (nullable, set by RenderSystem)
src/game/ecs/components/Velocity.ts — dx, dy, dz (units per second)
src/game/ecs/components/Collider.ts — shape, size, isStatic, isTrigger, collisionLayer, collisionMask
src/game/ecs/components/PlayerControlled.ts — marker with isGrounded, coyoteTimer, jumpBufferTimer
pnpm typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Five component files under src/game/ecs/components/. Transform (position/rotationY/scale), Velocity (dx/dy/dz), PlayerControlled (isGrounded/coyoteTimer/jumpBufferTimer), Collider (shape/size/isStatic/isTrigger/layer/mask), Renderable (meshConfig + nullable sceneHandle). Each exports a factory function. Renderable imports MeshConfig and SceneHandle from SceneManager (no THREE leak).

userStories.title: RenderSystem — ECS-to-Three.js bridge
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a system that synchronizes ECS entity transforms to Three.js scene objects each frame.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/systems/RenderSystem.ts implements System
Each frame: queries Transform + Renderable. New entities → sceneManager.addMesh(), store handle. Existing → sceneManager.updateTransform(). Destroyed → sceneManager.removeMesh().
No game logic — pure data sync
pnpm typecheck passes
userStories.notes: RenderSystem maintains a local Map<Entity, SceneHandle> as the source of truth for registered meshes. On each frame: new entities are addMesh()'d and handles stored; existing entities get updateTransform(); stale entities (destroyed or lost a component) are removeMesh()'d using the cached handle — no dependency on world.getComponent() for cleanup. Pure data sync, no game logic.

userStories.title: InputManager — keyboard and mouse input
userStories.description: As a player, I want responsive keyboard and mouse input so my character reacts immediately to WASD movement, jumping, and other actions.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/engine/InputManager.ts exposes getMovementIntent(): {x, z} (normalized), isActionPressed(action) (single-frame), isActionHeld(action), getMouseWorldPosition(): Vector3 | null, poll(), dispose()
WASD and Arrow Keys produce 8-direction intent; diagonals normalized
Spacebar = Jump (single press), E = Interact, M = Map, Ctrl+D = Debug
Mouse position raycast through SceneManager.screenToWorld()
Browser arrow-key scrolling prevented via e.preventDefault() on the scoped target
Listeners attach to the <canvas> element (not window) so they don't fire when the user is interacting with DOM UI; dispose() removes them on unmount
pnpm typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Canvas gets tabIndex=0 so it can receive keyboard events. Ctrl+D stored as synthetic 'CtrlD' key to avoid conflicting with movement 'D'. Arrow-key and Space scrolling prevented via e.preventDefault(). Listeners on canvas (not window) so DOM UI interactions don't bleed into game input. poll() clears pressedThisFrame each frame.

userStories.title: PhysicsEngine — gravity, ground detection, collision
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a lightweight physics engine for gravity, ground detection, and collision.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/engine/PhysicsEngine.ts exposes addBody(entity, config): BodyHandle, removeBody(handle), raycast(origin, direction, maxDist): RaycastHit | null, step(dt)
BodyConfig: shape, size, isStatic, isTrigger, collisionLayer, collisionMask
Gravity -12 u/s^2 on non-static, non-grounded bodies
Ground detection via downward raycast with 0.05u snap tolerance
Circle collision (0.4u radius, 0.1u skin width) with wall-sliding
Triggers detect overlap and emit events via EventBus; static bodies never move
pnpm typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Self-contained physics engine with internal body state. BodyHandle is a Symbol. Slab-method AABB raycast for ground detection. Circle-vs-box collision resolves via closest-point-on-box push (wall-sliding). Trigger overlaps tracked with 'entity:entity' key set; enter/exit events emitted via EventBus. Additional accessors (getPosition, setPosition, getVelocity, setVelocity, isBodyGrounded, getHandleByEntity) provided for MovementSystem integration.

userStories.title: MovementSystem — walk + jump with coyote and buffer
userStories.description: As a player, I want smooth walking and responsive jumping so controlling my character feels satisfying.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/systems/MovementSystem.ts implements System
Walk speed 4.5 u/s, accel 0.3s, decel 0.2s — sourced from runtimeConfig
Jump: 3.5u upward impulse on press, 1.2u apex
Air control: 70% horizontal retention
Coyote time 5 frames (~83ms) after walking off edges
Jump buffer 5 frames before landing
Mesh rotates to face 8-direction movement
Reads only from InputManager — no direct keyboard access
pnpm typecheck passes
userStories.notes: MovementSystem queries Transform+Velocity+PlayerControlled. Syncs transform from physics body (prev-frame result) at start. Coyote timer armed on grounded→airborne transition, ticked each airborne frame. Jump buffer armed on Jump press, consumed when canJump (grounded or coyote>0). Horizontal: per-axis accel/decel rates (walkSpeed/accel|decel time), target scaled by airControlFactor when airborne. Velocity written to physics body each frame; Y is owned by physics except on jump frames. Facing: atan2(intent.x, intent.z) → rotationY. moveToward() helper avoids float jitter at rest.

userStories.title: CameraController — isometric follow with lead
userStories.description: As a player, I want an isometric camera that smoothly follows my character with a slight lead in my movement direction.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/engine/CameraController.ts exposes follow(target: Entity), setMode('follow' | 'free'), update(dt), dispose()
Three.js OrthographicCamera at 45deg azimuth, 60deg elevation
Dynamic 2-3u offset in player facing direction (lerped)
Smooth follow with damping; scroll-wheel zoom
Clamps to map boundaries
free mode: WASD pans camera (used by Phase 4 map editor)
pnpm typecheck passes
userStories.notes: OrthographicCamera at 45° azimuth, 60° elevation sourced from runtimeConfig. follow()/setMode()/update(dt)/dispose() implemented. Smooth follow with configurable damping (5 units/s convergence). Lead offset lerped each frame via runtimeConfig.cameraLeadLerp toward player facing. Scroll-wheel zoom (minZoom=5, maxZoom=25). setMapBounds() clamps focusX/Z. panBy() enables free-mode WASD panning from Game orchestrator. Camera installed into SceneManager.camera on construction.

userStories.title: CollisionSystem — broad and narrow phase
userStories.description: As a developer, I need per-frame collision detection so entities interact physically with terrain and each other.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/systems/CollisionSystem.ts implements System
Queries Transform + Collider
Broad phase: AABB overlap. Narrow phase: circle-circle / circle-box
Pushes non-static bodies apart with skin-width sliding
Trigger overlaps emit events via EventBus (no physics response)
Layers/masks respected
pnpm typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CollisionSystem implements System. Broad phase: AABB overlap in XZ plane. Narrow phase: circle-circle (push apart by overlap+skinWidth, shared equally or one-sided) and circle-box (closest-point-on-AABB push). Trigger pairs tracked with pairKey (smaller entity first); trigger:enter/exit emitted via EventBus. Solid collision:enter/exit also emitted. Layers/masks filter: (A.layer & B.mask) !== 0 && (B.layer & A.mask) !== 0. Non-static bodies have Transform mutated in-place.

userStories.title: Drizzle schema for game tables (characters, gameSaves, debugOverrides)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the game's persistence tables defined in Drizzle so character, save, and debug data has a typed home.
acceptanceCriteria: src/server/db/schema.ts adds three tables using the existing createTable factory so the cat-herder_ prefix is preserved
characters: userId (varchar 256, primary key — one character per WorkOS user for MVP), shape (varchar — box/sphere/cylinder), colorHex (varchar 7), sizeScale (real), createdAt, updatedAt
gameSaves: userId (varchar 256, primary key), version (varchar — '0.1'), saveData (jsonb), createdAt, updatedAt
debugOverrides: userId (varchar 256, primary key), overrides (jsonb), updatedAt — reserved for PRD-03, never auto-applied on load
No RLS / pg_session_jwt / PostgREST setup. Authorization is enforced inside tRPC protectedProcedure by scoping every query to ctx.user.id.
userId matches the WorkOS user.id shape (user_01XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX)
pnpm db:push runs cleanly against the Neon dev database
pnpm typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Three tables added to schema.ts using createTable factory: characters (userId PK, shape, colorHex, sizeScale, timestamps), gameSaves (userId PK, version, saveData jsonb, timestamps), debugOverrides (userId PK, overrides jsonb, updatedAt). pnpm typecheck and pnpm build pass. pnpm db:push requires live Neon credentials — run manually to push to dev DB.

userStories.title: game tRPC router (getCharacter / upsertCharacter / getSave / upsertSave / deleteSave)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a game tRPC router so the browser engine can read/write character, save, and debug data without ever touching Postgres directly.
acceptanceCriteria: src/server/api/routers/game.ts exports a gameRouter registered in appRouter (src/server/api/root.ts)
All procedures use protectedProcedure. Every query/mutation filters by ctx.user.id — never trust a userId from input.
getCharacter() returns the caller's character or null
upsertCharacter(input: { shape, colorHex, sizeScale }) validated by Zod (shape enum, colorHex hex regex, sizeScale 0.5-2.0); upserts row
getSave() returns { version, saveData } | null
upsertSave(input: { version, saveData }) upserts; saveData validated as a generic JSON object (concrete shape lives in PRD-03)
deleteSave() deletes the caller's save row (used by debug menu in PRD-03)
Each procedure validates inputs with Zod and returns typed results to the client
pnpm typecheck passes; manual round-trip from /play confirms read/write
userStories.notes: gameRouter with getCharacter, upsertCharacter (Zod: shape enum, colorHex hex regex, sizeScale 0.5-2.0), getSave, upsertSave (saveData: z.record(z.string(), z.unknown()) for Zod v4), deleteSave, plus getDebugOverrides/upsertDebugOverrides for PRD-03. All protectedProcedure, all scoped to ctx.user.id. Drizzle upsert via onConflictDoUpdate. Registered as appRouter.game.

userStories.title: Character Creator UI (React overlay) backed by tRPC
userStories.description: As a new player, I want to customize my character's shape, color, and size so my avatar feels personal.
acceptanceCriteria: src/app/play/_components/CharacterCreator.tsx is a client component (React) that renders an HTML overlay
Shape picker: Block, Sphere, Cylinder with visual previews
Color picker: hex input + 8 preset swatches
Size slider: Small (0.8), Medium (1.0), Large (1.2)
Start Game button

On submit: calls api.game.upsertCharacter.useMutation() (tRPC), then dismisses the overlay and signals the engine to spawn the player entity with matching Renderable (shape/color/size) at the map spawn point
On /play mount: api.game.getCharacter.useQuery() decides whether to render the creator (no character) or hand control to the engine (character exists)
Character renders in scene as the chosen colored primitive
pnpm typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CharacterCreator.tsx: React overlay with shape picker (box/sphere/cylinder + SVG previews), 8 color swatches + custom color input, 3 size options (0.8/1.0/1.2). Uses api.game.upsertCharacter.useMutation(); calls onComplete(config) on success. GameCanvas.tsx updated: api.game.getCharacter.useQuery() hydrates character state; showCreator=true when not loading and no character; game starts once character is set (passed to Game constructor). Game.ts updated: PlayerCharacterConfig interface added; spawnPlayer() replaces player mesh with correct geometry+color+size; GameOpts accepts optional character.

userStories.title: MapManager and 60x60 test map
userStories.description: As a player, I want to spawn on a test map with varied terrain so I have a world to explore.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/maps/MapData.ts defines MapData: name, size (width/depth), terrain grid (2D TerrainCell array), water zones, spawn points, hidden terrain zones
TerrainCell: type (grass/dirt/stone/water), height, navigable
src/game/maps/MapManager.ts exposes loadMap(data), getTerrainAt(x, z), getHeightAt(x, z), getSpawnPoints()
loadMap() creates terrain entities (Transform + Renderable + Collider) rendered as colored planes/boxes per terrain type

src/game/maps/TestMap.ts defines a 60x60 unit map with grass (green), dirt (brown), stone (gray), a ~15x15u water zone (blue), 2-3u elevated platforms, player spawn at center, 2 cat spawn points, one hidden terrain area, and boundary walls
Player spawns at the player spawn point
pnpm typecheck passes
userStories.notes: MapData.ts: TerrainCell (type/height/navigable), SpawnPoint (x/z/role), MapData (name/size/terrain[][]/cellSize/spawnPoints). MapManager.ts: greedy-rectangle zone grouping (right-then-down expansion) creates one ECS entity per maximal same-type-same-height rectangle; flat terrain gets thin slab (0.2u) below y=0; elevated terrain box from 0 to height; boundary walls as 4 static box entities. TestMap.ts: 30×30 grid (cellSize=2) = 60×60u world; water zone, dirt path, stone platform (2u), hidden zone, two elevated grass platforms (2u and 3u), player spawn at (0,0,0). pnpm typecheck and pnpm build pass.

userStories.title: Game orchestrator with fixed-timestep loop and destroy()
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a top-level Game class that bootstraps all modules and runs the game loop inside the canvas.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/engine/Game.ts exposes constructor(canvas: HTMLCanvasElement, opts: { user: { id: string; firstName?: string }, trpc: GameTrpcAdapter }), start(): Promise<void>, pause(), resume(), destroy()
start() initializes modules in order: EventBus → SceneManager → InputManager → PhysicsEngine → World → Systems → CameraController → MapManager → UIManager
Fixed timestep (1/60s physics) with accumulator; rendering interpolates between physics states
Frame order: InputManager.poll() → MovementSystem → PhysicsEngine.step() → CollisionSystem → CameraController.update() → RenderSystem → UIManager.update() → SceneManager.render()
destroy() cancels the rAF loop, disposes SceneManager / InputManager / CameraController, and detaches every listener — required so React StrictMode and HMR don't leak WebGL contexts

GameTrpcAdapter is a small interface the orchestrator uses to call the game router; the engine never imports from ~/trpc/* directly. GameCanvas constructs the adapter (closing over api.game.* mutations) and injects it.
Maintains 60 FPS on a dev machine with the test map loaded
pnpm typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Game.ts fully rewritten: GameTrpcAdapter interface (upsertSave only for MVP auto-save); GameOpts adds trpc field; all modules init in order (EventBus→SceneManager→InputManager→PhysicsEngine→World→Systems→CameraController→MapManager→UIManager); fixed-timestep accumulator at FIXED_DT=1/60s with MAX_ACCUMULATOR=0.1s cap; frame order: poll→MovementSystem→PhysicsEngine.step→CollisionSystem→CameraController.update→RenderSystem→UIManager.update→SceneManager.render; spawnPlayer() creates ECS entity (Transform+Velocity+PlayerControlled+Renderable+Collider) + PhysicsEngine body; first call uses map spawn point, subsequent calls keep current position; triggerAutoSave() saves player position every CONFIG.autoSaveIntervalMs ms; destroy() cancels rAF, disposes all modules, clears EventBus. UIManager.ts stub created (US-018 fills it). GameCanvas.tsx updated: api.game.upsertSave.useMutation() wrapped in stable trpcAdapter via ref pattern; adapter injected into GameOpts.

userStories.title: Basic HUD shell — health and FPS
userStories.description: As a player, I need a minimal HUD showing my basic status so I have feedback during gameplay.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/ui/UIManager.ts mounts/unmounts plain DOM panels positioned over the canvas (no React inside the engine — keeps the loop framework-agnostic)
src/game/ui/HUD.ts renders placeholder hearts and an FPS counter (top-right, only when process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development')
HUD elements positioned to not obstruct gameplay
pnpm typecheck passes
userStories.notes: UIManager creates an overlay div (position:absolute;inset:0) appended to canvas.parentElement; sets parent to position:relative if static. HUD builds hearts panel (bottom-left, 3 × ♥) and FPS counter (top-right, only when process.env.NODE_ENV==='development'). FPS computed via 60-sample rolling average of dt values. dispose() removes overlay from DOM.

userStories.title: ESLint config and pnpm lint quality gate
userStories.description: As a developer, I need pnpm lint working so the CLAUDE.md quality gate passes and architectural rules can be enforced statically.
acceptanceCriteria: ESLint installed with the Next.js config (pnpm add -D eslint eslint-config-next @typescript-eslint/parser @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin) and a flat eslint.config.js (or compat shim) at the repo root
lint script added to package.json (next lint or equivalent)
no-restricted-imports rule blocks importing from three outside src/game/engine/SceneManager.ts and src/game/engine/CameraController.ts
pnpm lint passes against the current codebase
userStories.notes: eslint@9 + eslint-config-next@16 (flat config array) + @typescript-eslint/parser+plugin. eslint.config.js uses createRequire to load CJS eslint-config-next from ESM module. no-restricted-imports blocks `three` everywhere except SceneManager.ts and CameraController.ts via config-level ignores. GameCanvas.tsx fixed: upsertSaveRef synced via useEffect (not render body), savedCharacterConfig derived via useMemo instead of setState-in-effect. ESLint 9 required — v10 breaks eslint-plugin-react@7.37.5 (bundled in eslint-config-next) with contextOrFilename.getFilename API change.
